decisions.summary: I treated the GitHub issue body as the effective plan because this workspace started without PAN-971 planning artifacts or beads. I created issue-scoped beads for the remaining unresolved follow-up work so implementation could proceed through the normal inspectable one-bead workflow.

The async filesystem persistence and explicit slot PR target guidance described in PAN-971 were already present on main, so the first implementation bead focused only on the still-missing structured slot context. The slot prompt now embeds a machine-readable AgentTaskInput JSON block derived from the vBRIEF item, its blockers, and its acceptance criteria.

I implemented auto-advance as a persisted swarm-state option plus a lightweight background poller in the dashboard server. The dispatch path is now shared between manual POST requests and automatic wave progression so both modes use the same guardrails, worktree setup, prompt generation, and slot spawning behavior.

I covered the remaining PAN-971 gaps with direct route-helper tests instead of a fragile end-to-end harness. The swarm route now exposes narrow test internals so the suite can assert the structured AgentTaskInput payload, verify the prose guardrails stay in the slot prompt, and confirm the auto-advance poller persists next-wave state when a finished wave becomes eligible to continue.

hazards.summary: Swarm slot prompts now serve both humans and Deft-compatible agents, so the structured block must remain stable and must not remove the existing plain-language guardrails about bead closing and PR targets.
hazards.mitigation: Keep the JSON block additive, preserve the prose instructions, and cover the prompt shape with regression tests before finishing the issue.

hazards.summary: The auto-advance poller can repeatedly encounter the same finished wave when capacity is exhausted or a next-wave dispatch fails, which would otherwise cause duplicate launches or noisy retries.
hazards.mitigation: Keep per-issue in-flight guards around automatic dispatch, persist the current wave in swarm state, and leave failed next-wave attempts on the same state so a later poll can retry cleanly without double-spawning the successful case.

resumePoint.description: Regression coverage is implemented and locally verified with targeted Vitest, ESLint, and typecheck runs. Next, stage .pan/continue.json, src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts, and src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts, commit bead workspace-vuor, close it for inspection, then run the full PAN-971 verification gates before calling pan done.

sessionHistory.note: I reconstructed missing PAN-971 execution context from the issue body, created three issue-scoped beads, committed the structured slot context change, and recorded the remaining work for auto-advance and regression coverage.
I refactored swarm dispatch into a shared async path, added persisted auto-advance state plus a background poller, and wired the CLI flag that enables automatic next-wave launches.

I added a dedicated swarm route regression suite that locks the structured AgentTaskInput payload, the slot prompt guardrails, and the persisted auto-advance transition from one completed wave to the next, then verified the touched files with targeted Vitest, ESLint, and typecheck runs.
